topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The string quartet had just finished their final, soaring note, and the reception hall erupted into a wave of applause. I was trying to navigate the crowded dance floor, clinging to a lukewarm glass of champagne, when I saw him. Standing near the buffet, laughing with a group of people I vaguely recognized, was Mark. My stomach performed a sudden, violent flip. Every muscle in my jaw tightened. I immediately began scanning the room, searching for an escape route, my eyes darting everywhere but toward his vicinity. When our paths inevitably converged near the cake table, I managed a strained, overly bright smile, offering a quick, clipped greeting before executing a sharp pivot and retreating toward the relative safety of the balcony.

[story 2]

"Oh my god, is that...?" I whispered, my voice sounding far too high-pitched even to my own ears. Across the ballroom, bathed in the warm glow of string lights, stood Liam. He was undeniably there, looking impossibly happy next to his new spouse. My carefully constructed composure dissolved instantly. I felt a distinct heat rising up my neck, a flush that spread rapidly across my cheeks, making me acutely aware of the way my dress felt clinging to my skin. I fumbled with my napkin, my hands trembling slightly as I tried to appear engrossed in examining a floral arrangement, praying he wouldn't notice the complete internal upheaval I was experiencing.

[story 3]

I was deep in conversation with Sarah about the questionable canapés when I caught sight of him across the room. It was Alex. He was leaning against a pillar, looking effortlessly charming, and for a split second, the entire volume of the party seemed to drop away. A sudden, icy feeling washed over me, making my breath catch in my throat. I suddenly became hyper-focused on smoothing down the fabric of my skirt, a nervous habit I hadn't realized I possessed. When he finally turned his head in my direction, I felt a desperate urge to melt into the nearest shadow, my gaze dropping instantly to the polished floor tiles as if they held the secrets of the universe.
